name: Emperor Ling [Liu Hong]

sections.short-biography: Ruled the Han from 168 to 189. Ruled through the Yellow Turban Rebellion.
sections.details:
Traditional Chinese 
	靈帝[劉宏]


	 Simplified Chinese 
	灵帝[刘宏]


	 Pronunciation 
	Ling2di4 [liu2hong2] 


	 Pinyin (Tones) 
	Língdì [liúhóng]


	 Wade-Giles 
	Emperor Ling [Liu Hung]

	 Other Names 
	Liu Hong

	 Name Notes 
	In Chinese the name of the emperor comes before the use of the word emperor hence Ling (the name) Di (emperor).

The posthumous name of Ling can mean “Keen”, but it can also be interpreted as even though the state is in chaos, its roots have not been hurt. Fan Ye actually commented that the posthumous name was quite lenient for Emperor Ling.

sections.biography-sanguo-yanyi: Son of Emperor Huan. He placed all his trust with the eunuchs, in particular Zhang Rang, whom he referred to as a foster father. He had two sons, one from Empress He named Liu Bian and one from Lady Wang known as Liu Xian. Emperor Ling favored Xian and wished for him to succeed his throne. However, the Emperor died before he could see this through. Liu Bian succeeded Emperor Ling.

full_text:
Emperor Ling [Liu Hong] 靈帝[劉宏]
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Emperor Ling [Liu Hong] (靈帝[劉宏]). Ruled the Han from 168 to 189. Ruled through the Yellow Turban Rebellion. Emperor Ling [Liu Hong] was affiliated with the Han Dynasty. Emperor Ling [Liu Hong] lived during the Later Han Dynasty period of Chinese history.

Emperor Ling [Liu Hong]靈帝[劉宏]
LivedAD156–189
Affiliations
Han Dynasty
Short Biography
Ruled the Han from 168 to 189. Ruled through the Yellow Turban Rebellion.
Details
Traditional Chinese靈帝[劉宏]
Simplified Chinese灵帝[刘宏]
PronunciationLing2di4[liu2hong2]
Pinyin (Tones)Língdì [liúhóng]
Wade-GilesEmperor Ling [Liu Hung]
Other NamesLiu Hong
Name NotesIn Chinese the name of the emperor comes before the use of the word emperor hence Ling (the name) Di (emperor). The posthumous name of Ling can mean “Keen”, but it can also be interpreted as even though the state is in chaos, its roots have not been hurt. Fan Ye actually commented that the posthumous name was quite lenient for Emperor Ling.
Family and Relations
Sons
Emperor Shao [Liu Bian]
Emperor Xian
Emperor Xian [Liu Xie]
Literary Appearances
Romance of the Three Kingdoms:1–2,4–5,36–37,57,93
BiographySanguo Yanyi
Son of Emperor Huan. He placed all his trust with the eunuchs, in particular Zhang Rang, whom he referred to as a foster father. He had two sons, one from Empress He named Liu Bian and one from Lady Wang known as Liu Xian. Emperor Ling favored Xian and wished for him to succeed his throne. However, the Emperor died before he could see this through. Liu Bian succeeded Emperor Ling.
